title: European Rights Retention Community of Practice – Ninth Meeting
content:
When?

  • Date: 11 May 2026
  • Time: 12:00 CEST

What?

This Community of Practice is an online collaborative space designed to bring together professionals to discuss and tackle the challenges around rights retention strategies.

Save Your Spot!

Register now to secure your spot.

The Webinar

At this 9th meeting, Knowledge Rights 21 (KR21) partner SPARC Europe welcomes speakers from Ireland and the UK to share their experiences and perspectives:

  • Ireland: Susan Reilly – IReL (Irish Research e-Library)
  • UK: Anna Vernon – Jisc

Susan and Anna will share their perspectives on address

title: Copyright Maximalism Will Stifle a Research-Intensive Digital Economy
content:
Blog post by Benjamin White

Originally published on 26 March 2026 on the Times Higher Education

On 18 March, after more than two years of deliberating what to do about artificial intelligence and copyright, the UK government has announced the need to “gather more evidence on how copyright laws are impacting the development and deployment of AI across the economy and the economic benefits of reform”.

Perhaps the reason for this delay has been minsters’ growing realisation that the issues concerning AI and copyright are far more complicated than the “Big Music v Big Tech” paradigm being presented in the media. As this
